title: Central nervous system regulation of brown adipose tissue.

assets.abstract: Thermogenesis, the production of heat energy, in brown adipose tissue is a significant component of the homeostatic repertoire to maintain body temperature during the challenge of low environmental temperature in many species from mouse to man and plays a key role in elevating body temperature during the febrile response to infection. The sympathetic neural outflow determining brown adipose tissue (BAT) thermogenesis is regulated by neural networks in the CNS which increase BAT sympathetic nerve

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Bridge paper score=10.0: links Heat/Fire concept to mechanistic neuroscience
